commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core QMD v2 MCP query construction to keep raw lexical queries while normalizing word-internal hyphens only for semantic `vec`/`hyde` sub-searches, with focused tests and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main sends the raw hyphenated query to `lex`, `vec`, and `hyde`; QMD v2.0.1 rejects those hyphenated semantic queries; then OpenClaw falls back to builtin on the primary QMD error. I did not run a live QMD daemon in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This is a draft protected-label PR with a contributor-stated live-proof gap, so the next action is maintainer review after live QMD proof or an explicit proof waiver.

target.title: fix(memory): preserve qmd lexical search for hyphenated queries
